name: nomad-schema-plugin-run
documentation:

Schema definition for run section in NOMAD.

classes.name: runschema.run.AccessoryInfo
documentation:
Information that *in theory* should not affect the results of the calculations (e.g.,
timing).

classes.name: runschema.run.ProgramInfo
documentation:
Contains information on the program that generated the data, i.e. the program_name,
program_version, program_compilation_host and program_compilation_datetime as direct
children of this field.

classes.name: runschema.run.Program
documentation: Contains the specifications of the program.

classes.name: runschema.run.TimeRun
documentation: Contains information on timing information of the run.

classes.name: runschema.run.MessageRun
documentation: Contains warning, error, and info messages of the run.

classes.name: runschema.run.Run
documentation: Every section run represents a single call of a program.

classes.name: runschema.system.AtomsGroup
documentation:
Describes a group of atoms which may constitute a sub system as in the case of a
molecule.

classes.name: runschema.system.Atoms

methods.name: runschema.system.Atoms.to_ase
documentation:
Returns an ASE Object that represents the data in this section.

Arguments:
    raise_exp: Optional flag to raise an exception instead of return None, if
        the ASE object could not be created.

Returns:
    The ASE Object or None, if the ASE Object could not be created (e.g. due
    to missing data).

Describes the atomic structure of the physical system. This includes the atom
positions, lattice vectors, etc.

classes.name: runschema.system.Symmetry
documentation: Section containing information about the symmetry properties of the atomic system.

classes.name: runschema.system.Prototype
documentation: Information on the prototype corresponding to the current section.

classes.name: runschema.system.SpringerMaterial
documentation:
Contains results of classification of materials with the same formula according to
Springer Materials. These include material and compound classsification, formula,
id, and references from the Springer Materials database.

classes.name: runschema.system.Constraint
documentation: Section describing a constraint between arbitrary atoms.

classes.name: runschema.system.System
documentation:
Contains parameters describing a system of atomic configuration. These inclue the
compound name, atomic positions, lattice vectors, contraints on the atoms, etc.

classes.name: runschema.calculation.ScfInfo
documentation:
Contains information on the self-consistent field (SCF) procedure, i.e. the number of
SCF iterations (number_of_scf_iterations) or a section_scf_iteration section with
detailed information on the SCF procedure of specified quantities.

classes.name: runschema.calculation.AccessoryInfo
documentation:
Information that *in theory* should not affect the results of the calculations (e.g.,
timing).

classes.name: runschema.calculation.TimeInfo
documentation:
Stores information on the date and timings of the calculation. They are useful for,
e.g., debugging or visualization purposes.

classes.name: runschema.calculation.EnergyValue
documentation: This metadata stores an energy value.

classes.name: runschema.calculation.EnergyTypeReference
documentation: This metadata stores an energy used as reference point.

classes.name: runschema.calculation.ErrorEstimateContribution
documentation: An estimate of a partial quantity contributing to the error for a given quantity.

classes.name: runschema.calculation.Atomic
documentation:
Generic section containing the values and information reqarding an atomic quantity
such as charges, forces, multipoles.

classes.name: runschema.calculation.AtomicValues
documentation: Generic section containing information regarding the values of an atomic quantity.

classes.name: runschema.calculation.AtomicGroup
documentation:
Generic section containing the values and information reqarding a molecular or sub-molecular
quantity that is a function of an atomic group such as radius of gyration...

classes.name: runschema.calculation.AtomicGroupValues
documentation: Generic section containing information regarding the values of a trajectory property.

classes.name: runschema.calculation.EnergyEntry
documentation: Section describing a type of energy or a contribution to the total energy.

classes.name: runschema.calculation.Energy
documentation: Section containing all energy types and contributions.

classes.name: runschema.calculation.ForcesEntry
documentation: Section describing a contribution to or type of atomic forces.

classes.name: runschema.calculation.Forces
documentation: Section containing all forces types and contributions.

classes.name: runschema.calculation.StressEntry
documentation: Section describing a contribution to or a type of stress.

classes.name: runschema.calculation.Stress
documentation: Section containing all stress types and contributions.

classes.name: runschema.calculation.ChargesValue
documentation: Contains information on the charge on an atom or projected onto an orbital.

classes.name: runschema.calculation.Charges
documentation:
Section describing the charges on the atoms obtained through a given analysis
method. Also contains information on the orbital projection of charges.

classes.name: runschema.calculation.BandGapDeprecated
documentation:
Base class for breaking up circular dependencies between BandGap, Dos, and
BandStructure.

classes.name: runschema.calculation.BandEnergies

class_variables.name: runschema.calculation.BandEnergies.band_gap
documentation: TODO: check if this can be removed

This section describes the eigenvalue spectrum for a set of kpoints given by
band_energies_kpoints.

classes.name: runschema.calculation.BandStructure
documentation:
This section stores information on a band structure evaluation along one-dimensional
pathways in the $k$ or $q$ (reciprocal) space given in section_band_segment.
Eigenvalues calculated at the actual $k$-mesh used for energy_total evaluations,
can be found in the eigenvalues section.

classes.name: runschema.calculation.DosFingerprint
documentation:
Section for the fingerprint of the electronic density-of-states (DOS). DOS
fingerprints are a modification of the D-Fingerprints reported in Chem. Mater. 2015,
27, 3, 735–743 (doi:10.1021/cm503507h). The fingerprint consists of a binary
representation of the DOS, that is used to evaluate the similarity of materials based
on their electronic structure.

classes.name: runschema.calculation.DosValues
documentation: Section containing information regarding the values of the density of states (DOS).

classes.name: runschema.calculation.Dos
documentation:
Section containing information of an electronic-energy or phonon density of states
(DOS) evaluation per spin channel.

It includes the total DOS and the projected DOS values. We differentiate `species_projected` as the
projected DOS for same atomic species, `atom_projected` as the projected DOS for different
atoms in the cell, and `orbital_projected` as the projected DOS for the orbitals of each
atom. These are hierarchically connected as:

    atom_projected = sum_{orbitals} orbital_projected

    species_projected = sum_{atoms} atom_projected

    total = sum_{species} species_projected

classes.name: runschema.calculation.ElectronicStructureProvenance
documentation: Provenance information for electronic structure calculations.

classes.name: runschema.calculation.BandGap
documentation: Band gap information for each spin channel.

classes.name: runschema.calculation.MultipolesValues
documentation: Section containing the values of the multipoles projected unto an atom or orbital.

classes.name: runschema.calculation.MultipolesEntry
documentation:
Section describing a multipole term. The unit of the values are given by C * m ^ n,
where n = 1 for dipole, 2 for quadrupole, etc.

classes.name: runschema.calculation.Multipoles
documentation:
Section containing the multipoles (dipoles, quadrupoles, ...)
for each atom.

classes.name: runschema.calculation.Thermodynamics
documentation: Section containing results related to a thermodynamics calculation.
TODO remove this section

classes.name: runschema.calculation.Volumetric
documentation:
Section defining a set of volumetric data on a uniform real-space grid.
Kind should be specified if the data is not explicitly defined by a metainfo class.

classes.name: runschema.calculation.PotentialValue
documentation: Section containing the values of the potential evaluated on a uniform real-space grid.

classes.name: runschema.calculation.Potential
documentation: Section containing all potential types.

classes.name: runschema.calculation.Density
documentation: Section containing the values of the density evaluated on a uniform real-space grid.

classes.name: runschema.calculation.Spectra
documentation: Section containing the spectra properties.

classes.name: runschema.calculation.GreensFunctions
documentation: Green's functions properties in different time/frequency basis.

classes.name: runschema.calculation.VibrationalFrequenciesValues
documentation: Section describing a vibrational spectrum.

classes.name: runschema.calculation.VibrationalFrequencies
documentation: Section containing results related to vibrational frequencies.

classes.name: runschema.calculation.RadiusOfGyrationValues
documentation:
Section containing information regarding the values of
radius of gyration (Rg).

classes.name: runschema.calculation.RadiusOfGyration
documentation:
Section containing information about the calculation of
radius of gyration (Rg).

classes.name: runschema.calculation.MagneticShielding
documentation:
Section containing the information of magnetic shielding tensors. This is the nuclear
response of a material to shield the effects of an applied external field. It is:

    B_induced = - magnetic_shielding * B_external

The isotropic part can be calculated as 1 / 3 * Tr(magnetic_shielding).

See, e.g, https://pubs.acs.org/doi/10.1021/cr300108a.

classes.name: runschema.calculation.ElectricFieldGradient
documentation:
Section containing the information of electric field gradient tensors. These tensors
are relevant for nuclear magnetic responses and address the interaction between the
quadrupole moment of the nucleus and the electric field gradient (EFG) at the nucleus
position generated by the surrounding charges.

The eigenvalues of these tensors can be used to compute the quadrupolar coupling constant
and the asymmetry parameter.

See, e.g, https://pubs.acs.org/doi/10.1021/cr300108a.

classes.name: runschema.calculation.SpinSpinCoupling
documentation:
Section containing the information of spin-spin couplings. These are the indirect
interactions between 2 nuclear spins that arises from hyperfine interactions between
the nuclei and local electrons.

Synonyms:
    - IndirectSpinSpinCoupling

classes.name: runschema.calculation.MagneticSusceptibility
documentation:
Section containing the information of magnetic susceptibility tensor. Degree of
magnetization of a material in the presence of a magnetic field.

classes.name: runschema.calculation.BaseCalculation
documentation:
Contains computed properties of a configuration as defined by the corresponding
section system and with the simulation method defined by section method. The
references to the system and method sections are given by system_ref and method_ref,
respectively.

Properties derived from a group of configurations are not included in this section but
can be accessed in section workflow.

classes.name: runschema.calculation.ScfIteration
documentation:
Every scf_iteration section represents a self-consistent field (SCF) iteration,
and gives detailed information on the SCF procedure of the specified quantities.

classes.name: runschema.calculation.Calculation
documentation:
Every calculation section contains the values computed
during a *single configuration calculation*, i.e. a calculation performed on a given
configuration of the system (as defined in section_system) and a given computational
method (e.g., exchange-correlation method, basis sets, as defined in section_method).

The link between the current section calculation and the related
system and method sections is established by the values stored in system_ref and
method_ref, respectively.

The reason why information on the system configuration and computational method is
stored separately is that several *single configuration calculations* can be performed
on the same system configuration, viz. several system configurations can be evaluated
with the same computational method. This storage strategy avoids redundancies.

classes.name: runschema.method.Mesh
documentation:
Contains the settings for a sampling mesh.
Supports uniformly-spaced meshes and symmetry-reduced representations.

classes.name: runschema.method.LinePathSegment
documentation: Contains the settings for a single line path segment in a mesh.

classes.name: runschema.method.KMesh
documentation:
Contains the settings for a sampling mesh in 3D reciprocal space.
Supports uniformly-spaced meshes, line paths along high-symmetry points,
as well as symmetry-reduced and full representations.

classes.name: runschema.method.FrequencyMesh
documentation: Contains the settings for a sampling mesh in 1D frequency space, either real or imaginary.

classes.name: runschema.method.TimeMesh
documentation: Contains the settings for a sampling mesh in 1D time space, either real or imaginary.

classes.name: runschema.method.Scf

class_variables.name: runschema.method.Scf.threshold_density_change
documentation: TODO: unit
Section containing the parameters related to self consistency.

classes.name: runschema.method.HubbardKanamoriModel
documentation: Setup of the local Hubbard model.

classes.name: runschema.method.SingleElectronState

methods.name: runschema.method.SingleElectronState.set_j_quantum_number
documentation:
Given $l$ and $m_s$, set the total angular momentum $j$.
Return $j$, fi already set.

methods.name: runschema.method.SingleElectronState.set_mj_quantum_number
documentation: Given $m_l$ and $m_s$, set $m_j$. Return $m_j$, if already set.

methods.name: runschema.method.SingleElectronState.get_l_degeneracy
documentation: Return the multiplicity of the orbital angular momentum $l$.

methods.name: runschema.method.SingleElectronState.set_degeneracy
documentation:
Set the degeneracy based on how specifically determined the quantum state is.
This function can be triggered anytime to update the degeneracy.
An `AtomicOrbitalState` which supports fast notation for single-electron states.

classes.name: runschema.method.CoreHole

methods.name: runschema.method.CoreHole.set_occupation
documentation: Set the occupation based on the number of excited electrons.

Describes the quantum state of a single hole in an open-shell core state. This is the physical interpretation.
For modelling purposes, the electron charge excited may lie between 0 and 1. This follows a so-called Janak state.
Sometimes, no electron is actually, excited, but just marked for excitation. This is denoted as an `initial` state.
Any missing quantum numbers indicate some level of arbitrariness in the choice of the core hole, represented in the degeneracy.

classes.name: runschema.method.AtomParameters
documentation:
Contains method-related information about a kind of atom identified by label. This
allows the assignment of an atom-centered basis set or pseudopotential for different
atoms belonging to the same kind.

Through this section we use the wording "active" mainly for defining orbital-related
quantities. Active refers to the relevant orbital parameters in the atom.

classes.name: runschema.method.MoleculeParameters
documentation:
Contains method-related information about a kind of atom identified by label. This
allows the assignment of an atom-centered basis set or pseudopotential for different
atoms belonging to the same kind.

classes.name: runschema.method.Photon
documentation: Section containing the details of the photon field used for spectrum calculations.

classes.name: runschema.method.GaussianBasisGroup
documentation:
Section that describes a group of Gaussian contractions. Groups allow one to calculate
the primitive Gaussian integrals once for several different linear combinations of
them. This defines basis functions with radial part $f_i(r) = r^{l_i} \sum_{j} c_{i j}
A(l_i, \alpha_j) exp(-\alpha_j r^2)$ where $A(l_i, \alpha_j)$ is a the normalization
coefficient for primitive Gaussian basis functions. Here, $\alpha_j$ is defined in
gaussian_basis_group_exponents, $l_i$ is given in gaussian_basis_group_ls, and $c_{i
j}$ is given in gaussian_basis_group_contractions, whereas the radial part is given by
the spherical harmonics $Y_{l m}$.

This section is defined only if the original basis function uses Gaussian basis
functions, and the sequence of radial functions $f_i$ across all
section_gaussian_basis_group in section_basis_set_atom_centered should match the one
of basis_set_atom_centered_radial_functions.

classes.name: runschema.method.BasisSetAtomCentered
documentation:
This section describes the atom-centered basis set. The main contained information is
a short, non unique but human-interpretable, name for identifying the basis set
(short_name), a longer unique name, the atomic number of the atomic species the
basis set is meant for.

classes.name: runschema.method.OrbitalAPW
documentation: Definiton of a APW wavefunction per orbital.

classes.name: runschema.method.BasisSetMesh
documentation: All geometry-related information of the basis set (mesh).

classes.name: runschema.method.BasisSet
documentation:
This section contains all basis sets used to represent the wavefunction or electron
density.

classes.name: runschema.method.BasisSetContainer

class_variables.name: runschema.method.BasisSetContainer.scope
documentation: Change name to usage?
Container class for `BasisSet`

classes.name: runschema.method.Interaction
documentation: Section containing the parameters of a contribution to a force field model.

classes.name: runschema.method.Model
documentation:
Section containing the parameters of a force field model. If specified, the parameters
corresponding to the individual contributions to the model are given in contributions.
Otherwise, the parameters can also be found in a reference to the published model.

classes.name: runschema.method.Functional
documentation: Section containing the parameters of an exchange or correlation functional.

classes.name: runschema.method.XCFunctional
documentation:
Section describing the exchange-correlation functional used in the DFT calculation.
The name of the exchange-correlation functional is given by name and the reference to
the published functional is provided by reference. Other contributions to the
functional not covered by exchange, correlation or hybrid types may be specified in
contributions.

classes.name: runschema.method.DFT
documentation:
Section containing the various parameters that define a DFT calculation. These include
settings for the exchange correlation functionals, LDA+U, etc.

classes.name: runschema.method.TightBindingOrbital
documentation: Section to define an orbital including the name of orbital and shell number and the on-site energy.

classes.name: runschema.method.TwoCenterBond
documentation: Section to define a two-center approximation bond between two atoms.

classes.name: runschema.method.SlaterKosterBond
documentation: Section to define a two-center approximation bond between two atoms

classes.name: runschema.method.SlaterKoster
documentation: Section containing the various parameters that define a Slater-Koster

classes.name: runschema.method.xTB
documentation: Section containing the parameters pertaining to an extended tight-binding (xTB) calculation.

classes.name: runschema.method.Wannier
documentation: Section containing the various parameters that define a Wannier tight-binding method.

classes.name: runschema.method.TB
documentation:
Section containing the parameters pertaining to a tight-binding calculation. The TB
model can be derived from the Slater-Koster integrals, the xTB perturbation theory, or
the Wannier projection.

classes.name: runschema.method.HoppingMatrix
documentation:
Section containing the hopping/overlap matrix elements between N projected orbitals. This
is also the output of a TB calculation.

classes.name: runschema.method.LatticeModelHamiltonian
documentation: Section containing the parameters of the non-interacting parts of a lattice model Hamiltonian.

classes.name: runschema.method.CoreHoleSpectra
documentation:
Section containing the various parameters that define a calculation of core-hole spectra.
It can be within BSE as a "core" subsection.

classes.name: runschema.method.ExcitedStateMethodology
documentation:
Base class containing the common numerical parameters typical of excited-state
calculations.

classes.name: runschema.method.Screening
documentation:
Section containing the various parameters that define a screening calculation, as for
example, in RPA.

classes.name: runschema.method.GW
documentation: Section containing the various parameters that define a GW calculation.

classes.name: runschema.method.BSE
documentation: Section containing the various parameters that define a BSE calculation.

classes.name: runschema.method.DMFT
documentation: Section containing the various parameters that define a DMFT calculation

classes.name: runschema.method.NeighborSearching
documentation: Section containing the parameters for neighbor searching/lists during a molecular dynamics run.

classes.name: runschema.method.ForceCalculations
documentation:
Section containing the parameters for force calculations according to the referenced force field
during a molecular dynamics run.

classes.name: runschema.method.ForceField
documentation: Section containing the parameters pertaining to a force field calculation.

classes.name: runschema.method.Smearing
documentation:
Section containing the parameters related to the smearing of the electronic density of
states at the Fermi level.

classes.name: runschema.method.Electronic
documentation: Section containing the parameters related to the electronic structure.

classes.name: runschema.method.Method
documentation:
Section containing the various parameters that define the theory and the
approximations (convergence, thresholds, etc.) behind the calculation.
